cot_prefix: Okay, let's tackle this problem step by step. First, I need to figure out when Jennifer could have gone to the swimming pool based on the given information. The key here is to check the times when Jennifer was doing other activities and see when she was free. The swimming pool is closed after 10pm, so the latest she could go is before that. Let me list out the events and times: - Jennifer woke up at 5am. - Richard saw her at the office from 5am to 8am. - Samantha saw her at the library from 12pm to 1pm. - Betty saw her at the bike shop from 1pm to 2pm. - Sarah saw her at the electronics store from 2pm to 9pm. - Thomas saw her walking to the Statue of Liberty from 9pm to 10pm. So, the times she was busy are: - 5am to 8am (office) - 12pm to 1pm (library) - 1pm to 2pm (bike shop) - 2pm to 9pm (electronics store) - 9pm to 10pm (Statue of Liberty) The gaps between these activities might be when she was free. Let's check the possible gaps: 1. After waking up at 5am, she was at the office until 8am. So from 5am to 8am is occupied. The first possible free time would be after 8am. 2. From 8am until 12pm: Is there anything? The next activity is at the library from 12pm to 1pm. So between 8am and 12pm, she might have been free. Wait, unless there's something else. The problem doesn't mention anything else here. So 8am to 12pm is a possible window. 3. Then from 1pm to 2pm, she was at the bike shop. So between 12pm to 1pm, she was at the library, 1pm to 2pm at the bike shop. So between 12pm and 2pm, she's busy.
